items.title: Rare photos of 'enchanting' owl captured after happenstance sighting

children.content: A traditional owner on a Northern Australian island has captured rare photos of an "enchanting" masked owl, adding to the limited knowledge base surrounding the endemic and culturally significant bird.

image.altText: An owl with spotted feathers perched on a tree branch, looking directly at the camera.

items.title: King Charles pens 'very different' open letter after NT flood disaster

children.content: King Charles has written an open letter to Northern Territory residents following recent devastating flooding, saying the "increasingly catastrophic effects of climate change are surely a siren-call to the world for more urgent action".

image.altText: a close up of king charles, wrinkled and red face with a receding grey hairline

items.title: Man charged with murder over woman's 'suspicious' kangaroo crash death

children.content: NT police have charged a 33-year-old man with murder over the "suspicious death" of his female partner, who was found dead on an outback road over the weekend, after he alleged she was injured in a crash involving a kangaroo.

gallery.altText: A four-wheel-drive with damage to the front of the vehicle

thumbnail.altText: A four-wheel-drive with damage to the front of the vehicle

items.title: Man accused of failing to help his dying wife back behind bars

children.content: Kevin Stanischewski is accused of leaving his wife unconscious for hours after she allegedly told him she had drunk poison.

image.altText: A white, modernist, flat-fronted building with "supreme court" in capital letters above a wide entrance.

items.title: More than 1,000 fish killed in Darwin after iron ore pollution

children.content: More than 1,000 small fish die in Darwin Port after acidic run-off from an iron ore stockpile enters a containment pond following heavy rain.

image.altText: An aerial view of a ship anchored at a wharf part of the Darwin Port.

items.title: Family still waits to know how their loved one died, one year on

children.content: The family of Indigenous man Kumanjayi White has criticised the secrecy around the independent review into his death, which has been handed to NT Police and the DPP but not released publicly.

gallery.altText: An illustration of an Indigenous man with dark hair and a beard, standing on a red dirt landscape and wearing a yellow t-shirt.

thumbnail.altText: An illustration of an Indigenous man with dark hair and a beard, standing on a red dirt landscape and wearing a yellow t-shirt.

items.title: Remote community's bid to relocate progresses after '30-odd years of asking'

children.content: Flood-affected residents from Daly River are in talks with the NT government about moving the remote Aboriginal community to higher ground, with a church offering to donate land for the township's relocation.

image.altText: An aerial image shows a swollen river on the edge of a remote community, with brown water flooding local homes.

items.title: NT prosecutors considering appeal of four-year DV manslaughter sentence

children.content: A four-year prison term handed to a man who fatally stabbed in his wife on the Darwin Esplanade in 2023 could be appealed by prosecutors.

image.altText: Man in a blue shirt is interviewed for TV

items.title: Officers to take up arms on Darwin's 'dangerous' bus network

children.content: As the conflict in the Middle East continues, more people than ever are turning to public transport to escape the cost of driving — but in Darwin, that choice comes with a different price.

avatar.alt: A journalist wears a pattern blouse and serious facial expression.

items.title: NT's ambulance service fails for second time in a week

children.content: The Northern Territory's ambulances service has reached "critical system failure" for the second time in a week, as a St John NT director says the service is so under-resourced its operations are at risk of failure "all the time".

image.altText: A St John paramedic dressed in uniform and standing in front of an ambulance, it's centered on a logo emblazoned on their arm.

items.title: Darwin midwife's $1.1 million government contract under review

children.content: Darwin midwife Claire Marks was under investigation by the nursing and midwifery watchdog when she was handed a $1.1 million contract by the NT government, which is now reviewing the matter, as doctors continue to raise concerns, and the service brings on new staff.

image.altText: A woman in a brown dress looks at the camera.

items.title: 'A worker is going to be stabbed': Union airs Royal Darwin Hospital safety concerns

children.content: NT Health has rejected calls for a security review at Royal Darwin Hospital's busy emergency department amid threats against staff, with staff concerned "a worker is going to be stabbed".

gallery.altText: Staff wearing masks in emergency department at hospital

thumbnail.altText: Staff wearing masks in emergency department at hospital

avatar.alt: Jane Bardon in a blazer looking at the camera.

items.title: 'Never friends': Melania Trump criticises Epstein 'lies' in unusual address

children.content: In an unusual White House address, Melania Trump denies having a friendship with Jeffrey Epstein and complains about "mean-spirited" attempts to smear her.

avatar.alt: Brad Ryan is pictured in front of a news studio background, which shows a bridge in a city.

items.title: This couple has a fixed price contract to build their home — it's just gone up $120,000

children.content: Contractors, business owners and economists warn that thousands of construction industry businesses could go bust as a result of supply chain disruptions caused by the Iran War, unless the government intervenes.

image.altText: Housing construction

items.title: Long queues at EV chargers highlight gaps in charging infrastructure

children.content: Sales of electric vehicles have surged, but NSW government mapping shows regional areas are still lacking EV charging stations.

image.altText: Close up of electric vehicle being charged at a station in regional NSW.

items.title: Deep in a Ugandan forest, chimpanzees wage a lethal 'civil war'

children.content: A decades-long study reveals hundreds of chimpanzees waging a violent and deadly conflict against their former friends in their remote forest home.

image.altText: Looking through a frame of green leaves at one chimpanzee showing its teeth and another chimp watching on.

gallery.altText: a chimpanzee with its mouth open in the forest

thumbnail.altText: a chimpanzee with its mouth open in the forest

items.title: Friends, family thank the community for helping to find missing woman

children.content: The family and friends of a woman who was missing for a week in south-western Victoria have thanked the community after she was found safe and well.

image.altText: collage of four photos of a woman with black hair

items.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs's four-year prison sentence questioned in appeals court

children.content: Sean "Diddy" Combs's lawyers argue his four-year prison term for prostitution-related charges is too harsh.

image.altText: Sean Diddy Combs looking at something with sunglasses on

items.title: Racism erupts after Melbourne announces Little India plans

children.content: Social media posts about the announcement of a Little India precinct in Melbourne are flooded with racist comments, leaving some Indians with mixed feelings about the plan.

gallery.altText: Purva wearing a pink sari while speaking on stage at a panel

thumbnail.altText: Purva wearing a pink sari while speaking on stage at a panel

items.title: Australian role in Hormuz depends on permanent ceasefire, Marles says — as it happened

children.content: Australia has been involved in talks about assisting the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz but any action can only take place if a ceasefire becomes permanent, Richard Marles says.

image.altText: Close up of Richard Marles wearing a suit and tie.

items.title: ASX 200 and Aussie dollar down, Brent crude oil up

children.content: The Australian market is set for a disappointing end to the trading week, finding itself in negative territory, despite US stocks rising overnight. Follow the day's events and insights as they happen with our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A stock market watcher stands in front of an ASX board.

items.title: Euphoria ushered in a new generation of A-listers. Now, it's finally back

children.content: Sam Levinson's controversial teen series has had to overcome four years of actor deaths, character departures and on-set tension to make it to season three.

children.content: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

children.content: Expert tips on getting the most out of your favourite bath towels and when it might be time to replace them.

items.title: 'People assume we're not real': MPs open up about mental health challenges

children.content: A national study has found 70 per cent of politicians feel their job is affecting their mental health. The ABC spoke to three Tasmanian MPs, including the state's premier, about their experiences.

gallery.altText: Jeremy Rockliff leans against a ute at his farm.

thumbnail.altText: Jeremy Rockliff leans against a ute at his farm.

items.title: Super members blindsided by change in fund terms and conditions

children.content: A superannuation investment option spruiked as offering "choice and flexibility" has been shut down without proper notification, leaving members less than two months to sell all their assets.

image.altText: Lauren sits on the couch with laptop in the lap, her hair in a high bun

items.title: Sexual misconduct now publicly listed on 100 health practitioners' records

children.content: More than 100 past and current practitioners have had findings of sexual misconduct added to their public-facing records under new reforms to promote transparency.

image.altText: Blurred image of a doctor with a patient at the Curtin Medical School with a door marked "clinical skills" in the foreground.

items.title: The NDIS's 'social licence' isn't just at risk — it's close to collapse

children.content: The NDIS's reputation isn't just at risk of coming under pressure, it's well and truly crumbling, write Nas Campanella and Evan Young.

image.altText: Mark Butler standing in a corridor in a suit

avatar.alt: A vision impaired woman with long hair in a blue dress holding a cane

items.title: Man accused of stealing $1.1 million of diesel fuel from unmanned depots

children.content: Police will allege a 48-year-old man used stolen fuel cards to obtain the diesel from unmanned depots over a 12-month period.

image.altText: Containers stacked at Greenbank

items.title: The fourth-year med student from Broken Hill who won the Stawell Gift

children.content: Olufemi Komolafe is Broken Hill's first winner of the Stawell Gift since 1949. The 21-year-old sprinter and med student took out the men’s final at the iconic 2026 foot race in Western Victoria over the Easter long weekend.

gallery.altText: A young male athelete smiles while holding a large golden trophy.

thumbnail.altText: A young male athelete smiles while holding a large golden trophy.

items.title: Travelling to Europe? New biometric border rules start today

children.content: Australians travelling to Europe will be fingerprinted and face-scanned at the border from today, as authorities activate a sweeping new digital system designed to track who enters and leaves the continent.

image.altText: A man operates an automated EES kiosk at a european border

items.title: From Apollo to Artemis, the lunar legacy of the Parkes 'Dish' continues

children.content: The Parkes Observatory — also known as The Dish — continues a legacy of supporting lunar missions that started more than 50 years ago, helping Artemis II go where humanity has not since 1972.

image.altText: The moons surface with earth in the background

items.title: Herb in cold and flu products linked to life-threatening anaphylaxis

children.content: The TGA says people taking popular cold and flu supplements face the risk of life-threatening anaphylaxis from a herbal ingredient.

image.altText: A woman sits at a table with boxes of medication in front of her

items.title: Animals on wildlife market more likely to carry human-infecting disease, report finds

children.content: Of the 2,079 mammal species traded worldwide, 41 per cent share at least one pathogen with humans, according to new data released today.

image.altText: four birds sit on a ledge inside a market

items.title: Sister pushes to raise awareness of male eating disorders after tragic loss

children.content: Joe Cappello was 25 years old when he died after a 12-year struggle, and the statistics show many more men at are risk.
